dependencies: Highly dependent on information technology systems and networks operated by third parties
Reliance on unaffiliated sub-custodians to a greater degree than certain competitors
Dependence on third-party vendors for data processing, recording and monitoring transactions, online banking interfaces and services, and network access
risks: Failure of third-party vendors to perform can result in losses
Risks from vendors' vendors (indirect third-party dependencies)
Third-party vendor failures or service disruptions
Concentration risk with critical service providers
Disruptions in service from key vendors could negatively affect ability to deliver products and services
Replacing third-party vendors could create significant delay and expense
Third-party vendors may not comply with servicing and contractual obligations including information security

regulatory_risks: Interchange fee regulations
Enhanced prudential standards for large banking organizations
Restrictions on transactions with affiliates
Extensive regulation and supervision under state and federal laws in the United States and in each jurisdiction where business is conducted
AIFMD and UCITS depositary requirements
OFAC sanctions and economic sanctions programs
Anti-Money Laundering (AML) and Bank Secrecy Act requirements
Resolution planning requirements under Section 165(d) of Dodd-Frank Act
Restrictions on subsidiary dividends and capital distributions
Tax law changes and compliance
Orderly liquidation authority under Dodd-Frank Act
Regulatory divergence between UK and EU post-Brexit
Community Reinvestment Act (CRA) requirements and new CRA final rule
Dodd-Frank Act compliance requirements
Data privacy and security regulations (including GDPR)
Net Stable Funding Ratio (NSFR) requirements
Volcker Rule restrictions on proprietary trading and covered fund investments
Shareholder Rights Directive (SRD II)
Basel III capital requirements and potential changes from Basel III Endgame Proposal
Liquidity Coverage Ratio (LCR) requirements
FDIC special assessment related to 2023 bank failures
Data privacy and security regulations (GDPR, UK GDPR, CCPA, PIPL)
Securities Financing Transactions and Reuse of Collateral Regulation (SFTR)
Sanctions compliance (U.S., EU, and other jurisdictions)
Comprehensive regulation of derivatives businesses (CFTC and SEC rules)
Resolution planning requirements
CCAR (Comprehensive Capital Analysis and Review) stress testing
Liquidity Coverage Ratio (LCR) and Net Stable Funding Ratio (NSFR) requirements
Sustainable Finance Disclosure Regulations (SFDR)
Central Securities Depositories Regulation (CSDR)
Capital planning and stress testing requirements (CCAR and DFAST)
FDIC deposit insurance and assessment requirements
Deposit Guarantee Schemes Directive (DGSD)
Enhanced prudential standards applicable to Category II banking organizations
Capital adequacy requirements under Basel III framework
Category II institution regulatory standards
EU and UK prudential regulatory frameworks (CRD, CRR, CRD VI, CRR III)
Markets regulation (MIFID, MIFIR, EMIR)
Fifth EU Money Laundering Directive (MLD5) and Sixth EU Money Laundering Directive
Potential changes under new U.S. presidential administration and Congress
FDIC insurance requirements and special assessments
Fiduciary duty standards and regulations
European Union Bank Recovery and Resolution Directive (BRRD) and UK special resolution regime
Anti-money laundering (AML) compliance
Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) regulations
Prompt corrective action requirements
Consumer protection laws and regulations (CFPB oversight)
Community Reinvestment Act (CRA) obligations
EU Benchmarks Regulation (BMR)
Long-term debt and clean holding company requirements (proposed rule)
Foreign regulatory requirements in multiple jurisdictions
Federal Reserve and state banking regulations
AI regulation (evolving laws and regulations on artificial intelligence)
Broker-dealer and investment adviser regulations (SEC, FINRA, MSRB)
Climate-related disclosure requirements
Basel III Endgame Proposal (proposed rule to implement Basel III endgame agreement)
EU Taxonomy Regulations

sanction_risks: Increased credit exposure to subcustodians in sanctioned jurisdictions
Russia sanctions impact - cash balances denominated in Russian rubles subject to distribution restrictions ($1.1 billion as of December 31, 2024)
Sanctions compliance requirements across multiple jurisdictions
Trade policy changes including tariffs and retaliatory tariffs
Accumulation of cash deposits with Russian subcustodians subject to restrictions
Compliance with financial and economic sanctions regimes
Potential sovereign debt defaults and related financial market stresses
Military conflict between Ukraine and Russian Federation
Economic sanctions and embargo programs administered by OFAC
Russia sanctions restricting ability to access or transfer cash or securities held through subcustodians
